heading: Organization
label: 7254 Organization
notes: Amendments1999—Subsec. (d). inserted heading and amended text of subsec. (d) generally. Prior to amendment, text read as follows: “In the event of a vacancy in the position of chief judge of the Court, the associate judge senior in service on the Court shall serve as acting chief judge unless the President designates one of the other associate judges to serve as acting chief judge, in which case the judge so designated shall serve as acting chief judge.”Pub. L. 106–1171998—Subsec. (a). substituted “Court of Appeals for Veterans Claims” for “Court of Veterans Appeals”.Pub. L. 105–3681991— renumbered as this section.Pub. L. 102–40section 4054 of this titleSubsecs. (d), (e). amended section as in effect immediately before the enactment of , and amended section, identically, by redesignating the second subsec. (d), relating to authority to administer oaths as, (e).Pub. L. 102–54Pub. L. 102–40Pub. L. 102–821989—Subsec. (d). added subsec. (d) relating to authority to administer oaths.Pub. L. 101–237added subsec. (d) relating to acting chief judge in event of vacancy.Pub. L. 101–94

children.label: a
children.text: The Court of Appeals for Veterans Claims shall have a seal which shall be judicially noticed.

children.label: b
children.text: The Court may hear cases by judges sitting alone or in panels, as determined pursuant to procedures established by the Court. Any such panel shall have not less than three judges. The Court shall establish procedures for the assignment of the judges of the Court to such panels and for the designation of the chief of each such panel.

A majority of the judges of the Court shall constitute a quorum for the transaction of the business of the Court. A vacancy in the Court shall not impair the powers or affect the duties of the Court or of the remaining judges of the Court.

A majority of the judges of a panel of the Court shall constitute a quorum for the transaction of the business of the panel. A vacancy in a panel of the Court shall not impair the powers or affect the duties of the panel or of the remaining judges of the panel.

children.heading: .—Precedence of Judges
children.label: d .—Precedence of Judges
children.text: The chief judge of the Court shall have precedence and preside at any session that the chief judge attends. The other judges shall have precedence and preside according to the seniority of their original commissions. Judges whose commissions bear the same date shall have precedence according to seniority in age.

children.label: e
children.text: Judges of the Court shall have the authority to administer oaths.
